seoTitle: How to Delete Your n8n Cloud Account
metaDescription: Learn how to safely back up your workflows and permanently delete your n8n cloud account, including credentials and data.
intro: If you're closing out an n8n cloud account, whether you're switching hosting providers or just cleaning up an old test setup, it's important to back up anything you might need first, since deletion wipes your instance and its data permanently. This guide walks through the account closure process step by step.

steps.heading: Export your workflows first
steps.body: Before doing anything else, download copies of your active workflow configurations as JSON backups in case you need them later.

steps.heading: Open your account dashboard
steps.body: Sign into your n8n cloud workspace and navigate to your account settings console.

steps.heading: Go to Profile Settings
steps.body: Find the main profile settings panel where core account security actions live.

steps.heading: Review your active instances
steps.body: Check your cloud workspace capacity and confirm you're closing the correct account before proceeding.

steps.heading: Locate the delete account option
steps.body: Find the advanced profile management section and select the delete account control.

steps.heading: Confirm the deletion
steps.body: Review the warning prompt carefully, then confirm your identity to permanently delete your account and wipe its associated data.

faq.question: Can I recover my account after deleting it?
faq.answer: No, account deletion is permanent, which is why exporting your workflows as backups beforehand is strongly recommended.

faq.question: Will deleting my account cancel my billing?
faq.answer: Deleting your account should stop future charges, but it's worth checking your billing section separately to confirm any subscription is fully canceled.

faq.question: Does deleting my account remove my API tokens too?
faq.answer: Yes, the deletion process clears associated data structures, API tokens, and log histories tied to that account.

faq.question: What happens to active workflows when I delete my account?
faq.answer: Any active production workflows and their background instances stop running once the account and its data are removed.
